# Curator Receipt

## Decision

Discarded because the source excerpt does not show enough empirical substance to justify institute admission.

## Why

This appears to be a research blog post announcing a tool and asserting strong outcomes, but the visible source material is mostly explanatory and promotional. It claims code evolution improved Vet and more than doubled reasoning performance on ARC-AGI tasks, yet the excerpt does not include concrete benchmark tables, experiment details, production telemetry, incident facts, or other visible evidence payload sufficient for admission. Under Curator law, uncertainty and thin evidence mean reject.

## Limits
